function.name: patch
function.description:
Targeted find-and-replace edits in files. Use this instead of sed/awk in terminal. Uses fuzzy matching (9 strategies) so minor whitespace/indentation differences won't break it. Returns a unified diff. Auto-runs syntax checks after editing.

Replace mode (default): find a unique string and replace it.
Patch mode: apply V4A multi-file patches for bulk changes.

mode.description: Edit mode: 'replace' for targeted find-and-replace, 'patch' for V4A multi-file patches

path.description: File path to edit (required for 'replace' mode)

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'replace' mode). Must be unique in the file unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'replace' mode). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: Replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false)

patch.description:
V4A format patch content (required for 'patch' mode). Format:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: path/to/file
@@ context hint @@
 context line
-removed line
+added line
*** End Patch

function.name: process
function.description: Manage background processes started with terminal(background=true). Actions: 'list' (show all), 'poll' (check status + new output), 'log' (full output with pagination), 'wait' (block until done or timeout), 'kill' (terminate), 'write' (send raw stdin data without newline), 'submit' (send data + Enter, for answering prompts), 'close' (close stdin/send EOF).

action.description: Action to perform on background processes

session_id.description: Process session ID (from terminal background output). Required for all actions except 'list'.

data.description: Text to send to process stdin (for 'write' and 'submit' actions)

timeout.description: Max seconds to block for 'wait' action. Returns partial output on timeout.

offset.description: Line offset for 'log' action (default: last 200 lines)

limit.description: Max lines to return for 'log' action

function.name: read_file
function.description: Read a text file with line numbers and pagination. Use this instead of cat/head/tail in terminal. Output format: 'LINE_NUM|CONTENT'. Suggests similar filenames if not found. Use offset and limit for large files. Reads exceeding ~100K characters are rejected; use offset and limit to read specific sections of large files. NOTE: Cannot read images or binary files — use vision_analyze for images.

path.description: Path to the file to read (absolute, relative, or ~/path)

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed, default: 1)

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read (default: 500, max: 2000)

function.name: search_files
function.description:
Search file contents or find files by name. Use this instead of grep/rg/find/ls in terminal. Ripgrep-backed, faster than shell equivalents.

Content search (target='content'): Regex search inside files. Output modes: full matches with line numbers, file paths only, or match counts.

File search (target='files'): Find files by glob pattern (e.g., '*.py', '*config*'). Also use this instead of ls — results sorted by modification time.

pattern.description: Regex pattern for content search, or glob pattern (e.g., '*.py') for file search

target.description: 'content' searches inside file contents, 'files' searches for files by name

path.description: Directory or file to search in (default: current working directory)

file_glob.description: Filter files by pattern in grep mode (e.g., '*.py' to only search Python files)

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 50)

offset.description: Skip first N results for pagination (default: 0)

output_mode.description: Output format for grep mode: 'content' shows matching lines with line numbers, 'files_only' lists file paths, 'count' shows match counts per file

context.description: Number of context lines before and after each match (grep mode only)

function.name: terminal
function.description:
Execute shell commands on a Linux environment. Filesystem usually persists between calls.

Do NOT use cat/head/tail to read files — use read_file instead.
Do NOT use grep/rg/find to search — use search_files instead.
Do NOT use ls to list directories — use search_files(target='files') instead.
Do NOT use sed/awk to edit files — use patch instead.
Do NOT use echo/cat heredoc to create files — use write_file instead.
Reserve terminal for: builds, installs, git, processes, scripts, network, package managers, and anything that needs a shell.

Foreground (default): Commands return INSTANTLY when done, even if the timeout is high. Set timeout=300 for long builds/scripts — you'll still get the result in seconds if it's fast. Prefer foreground for short commands.
Background: Set background=true to get a session_id. Two patterns:
  (1) Long-lived processes that never exit (servers, watchers).
  (2) Long-running tasks with notify_on_complete=true — you can keep working on other things and the system auto-notifies you when the task finishes. Great for test suites, builds, deployments, or anything that takes more than a minute.
For servers/watchers, do NOT use shell-level background wrappers (nohup/disown/setsid/trailing '&') in foreground mode. Use background=true so Hermes can track lifecycle and output.
After starting a server, verify readiness with a health check or log signal, then run tests in a separate terminal() call. Avoid blind sleep loops.
Use process(action="poll") for progress checks, process(action="wait") to block until done.
Working directory: Use 'workdir' for per-command cwd.
PTY mode: Set pty=true for interactive CLI tools (Codex, Claude Code, Python REPL).

Do NOT use vim/nano/interactive tools without pty=true — they hang without a pseudo-terminal. Pipe git output to cat if it might page.

command.description: The command to execute on the VM

background.description: Run the command in the background. Two patterns: (1) Long-lived processes that never exit (servers, watchers). (2) Long-running tasks paired with notify_on_complete=true — you can keep working and get notified when the task finishes. For short commands, prefer foreground with a generous timeout instead.

timeout.description: Max seconds to wait (default: 180, foreground max: 600). Returns INSTANTLY when command finishes — set high for long tasks, you won't wait unnecessarily. Foreground timeout above 600s is rejected; use background=true for longer commands.

workdir.description: Working directory for this command (absolute path). Defaults to the session working directory.

pty.description: Run in pseudo-terminal (PTY) mode for interactive CLI tools like Codex, Claude Code, or Python REPL. Only works with local and SSH backends. Default: false.

notify_on_complete.description: When true (and background=true), you'll be automatically notified exactly once when the process finishes. **This is the right choice for almost every long-running task** — tests, builds, deployments, multi-item batch jobs, anything that takes over a minute and has a defined end. Use this and keep working on other things; the system notifies you on exit. MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE with watch_patterns — when both are set, watch_patterns is dropped.

watch_patterns.description: Strings to watch for in background process output. HARD RATE LIMIT: at most 1 notification per 15 seconds per process — matches arriving inside the cooldown are dropped. After 3 consecutive 15-second windows with dropped matches, watch_patterns is automatically disabled for that process and promoted to notify_on_complete behavior (one notification on exit, no more mid-process spam). USE ONLY for truly rare, one-shot mid-process signals on LONG-LIVED processes that will never exit on their own — e.g. ['Application startup complete'] on a server so you know when to hit its endpoint, or ['migration done'] on a daemon. DO NOT use for: (1) end-of-run markers like 'DONE'/'PASS' — use notify_on_complete instead; (2) error patterns like 'ERROR'/'Traceback' in loops or multi-item batch jobs — they fire on every iteration and you'll hit the strike limit fast; (3) anything you'd ever combine with notify_on_complete. When in doubt, choose notify_on_complete. MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE with notify_on_complete — set one, not both.

path.description: Path to the file to write (will be created if it doesn't exist, overwritten if it does)

content.description: Complete content to write to the file

{"path": "/home/marco/.hermes/skills/trading/dyor-onchain-analytics/skill.md", "content": "# dyor-onchain-analytics

**Category:** trading / onchain-analytics
**Do Yo Own Research — Onchain Analytics Skill**

Real-time and historical onchain analytics for crypto trading: whale tracking, wallet profiling via DeBank/Footprint, DEX trade flow, smart money detection, and token holder analytics.

---

## Capabilities

### Whale Tracking
- Monitor large transactions (configurable threshold in USD)
- Track sudden token accumulation/distribution patterns
- Alert on whale transfers between exchanges and wallets
- Historical whale activity replay for pattern analysis

### DeBank Integration
- Portfolio tracking for any ENS or 0x address
- DeBank Pro API for professional data access
- Asset allocation, DeFi positions, transaction history
- Gas spending analytics

### Footprint Analytics Integration
- NFT floor prices and collection analytics
- DEX liquidity data (Gmx, dYdX, etc.)
- Wallet labeling and cluster analysis
- Cross-chain data (Ethereum, BSC, Polygon, Arbitrum, etc.)

### DEX Data & Trade Flow
- Real-time trade monitoring on Uniswap, SushiSwap, Curve
- Buy/sell pressure indicators
- DEX liquidity changes and pool creation alerts
- Sandwich attack detection heuristics

### Smart Money Detection
- Identify known smart money wallets (e.g.,abit of Dragonberries, etc.)
- Track institutional-grade wallets
- Copy-trading signal generation
- Historical P&L of tracked wallets

---

## API Keys Required

```env
DEBANK_API_KEY=           # Free tier: 1000 calls/day
FOOTPRINT_API_KEY=       # Free tier available
ETHERSCAN_API_KEY=       # Free, multiple keys OK
ALCHEMY_API_KEY=         # Free tier, optional
```

---

## Usage Examples

```
@skill dyor-onchain-analytics

> Track whale activity for 0x...abc (any Ethereum address)
> Show DeBank portfolio for vitalik.eth
> What are the top smart money wallets holding $PEPE?
> Monitor Uniswap v3 USDC/ETH pool for large trades
> Get token holder count and concentration for 0x...xyz
> Alert me when a whale buys >$500k of $XYZ
```

---

## Pitfalls

- **Rate limits**: DeBank free tier is 1000 req/day — cache aggressively
- **Address labels**: DeBank labels are community-sourced, verify independently
- **Whale threshold**: Adjust per token market cap (set too low = noise, too high = missed signals)
- **DEX data lag**: Uniswap subgraphs have ~15min delay on historical queries
- **Wallet clustering**: Heuristic-based; not always accurate for layered privacy
- **Cross-chain**: Footprint covers multiple chains but depth varies significantly
- **Smart money**: Past performance ≠ future results; wallets can exit and reposition

---

## Files

```
dyor-onchain-analytics/
├── skill.md
├── src/
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── whale_tracker.py
│   ├── debank_client.py
│   ├── footprint_client.py
│   └── dex_data.py
└── requirements.txt
```

---

## Architecture

```
onchain_analytics/
├── WhaleTracker       # Ethereum/wallet-level tx monitoring
├── DeBankClient       # Portfolio & DeFi position queries
├── FootprintClient    # NFT, cross-chain, labeling data
└── DexDataProvider    # DEX trade flow & liquidity
```

All clients return normalized Python dicts. Errors raise `OnchainAnalyticsError`.

{"path": "/home/marco/.hermes/skills/trading/dyor-onchain-analytics/src/whale_tracker.py", "content": """"
Whale Tracker — monitor large transactions and wallet movements.
Uses Etherscan API / Alchemy for tx fetching.
"""

import os
import time
import logging
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Optional, List, Dict, Any
from decimal import Decimal

import requests

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

ETHERSCAN_API_KEY = os.getenv("ETHERSCAN_API_KEY", "")
ALCHEMY_API_KEY = os.getenv("ALCHEMY_API_KEY", "")

# Default USD threshold for "whale" classification
DEFAULT_WHALE_THRESHOLD_USD = 100_000


@dataclass
class WhaleAlert:
    """Represents a single whale transaction."""
    tx_hash: str
    block_number: int
    from_address: str
    to_address: str
    value_eth: float
    value_usd: float
    timestamp: int  # unix epoch
    token_symbol: Optional[str] = None
    token_address: Optional[str] = None
    is_suspicious: bool = False
    notes: str = ""


class WhaleTracker:
    """
    Track large onchain transactions.
    
    Usage:
        tracker = WhaleTracker()
        alerts = tracker.get_whale_alerts(token_address="0x...", min_usd=500_000)
        tracker.track_address("0x...", label="Binance Hot Wallet")
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        ethereum_rpc: Optional[str] = None,
        etherscan_api_key: Optional[str] = None,
        alchemy_api_key: Optional[str] = None,
        cache_ttl: int = 60,
    ):
        self.etherscan_key = etherscan_api_key or ETHERSCAN_API_KEY
        self.alchemy_key = alchemy_api_key or ALCHEMY_API_KEY
        self.cache_ttl = cache_ttl
        self._cache: Dict[str, tuple[float, Any]] = {}
        self._tracked_addresses: Dict[str, str] = {}  # address -> label

        if self.alchemy_key:
            self.rpc = ethereum_rpc or f"https://eth-mainnet.g.alchemy.com/v2/{self.alchemy_key}"
        else:
            self.rpc = ethereum_rpc or "https://eth.llamarpc.com"

    # ─── Address tracking ────────────────────────────────────────────────

    def track_address(self, address: str, label: str = "") -> None:
        """Add an address to the watchlist."""
        self._tracked_addresses[address.lower()] = label or "tracked_wallet"

    def untrack_address(self, address: str) -> None:
        self._tracked_addresses.pop(address.lower(), None)

    def get_tracked_addresses(self) -> Dict[str, str]:
        return self._tracked_addresses.copy()

    # ─── Whale detection ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

    def get_whale_alerts(
        self,
        token_address: Optional[str] = None,
        min_usd: float = DEFAULT_WHALE_THRESHOLD_USD,
        hours_back: int = 24,
        limit: int = 100,
    ) -> List[WhaleAlert]:
        """
        Fetch recent large transactions involving a token.
        
        Args:
            token_address: Optional ERC20 token address. If None, only ETH txs.
            min_usd: Minimum USD value to flag as whale.
            hours_back: How far back to look.
            limit: Max number of results (Etherscan caps at 10000 but we default 100).
        
        Returns:
            List of WhaleAlert objects.
        """
        cache_key = f"whale_alerts:{token_address}:{min_usd}:{hours_back}"
        if self._is_cached(cache_key):
            return self._get_cached(cache_key)

        if self.etherscan_key:
            alerts = self._fetch_via_etherscan(token_address, min_usd, hours_back, limit)
        else:
            logger.warning("No Etherscan API key — falling back to Alchemy/alchemy-like RPC")
            alerts = self._fetch_via_rpc(token_address, min_usd, hours_back, limit)

        self._set_cache(cache_key, alerts)
        return alerts

    def _fetch_via_etherscan(
        self,
        token_address: Optional[str],
        min_usd: float,
        hours_back: int,
        limit: int,
    ) -> List[WhaleAlert]:
        """Use Etherscan API for token transfer events."""
        alerts: List[WhaleAlert] = []

        if token_address:
            # ERC20 token transfers
            endpoint = "https://api.etherscan.io/api"
            params = {
                "module": "tokentx",
                "action": "tokentx",
                "contractaddress": token_address,
                "sort": "desc",
                "page": 1,
                "offset": limit,
                "apikey": self.etherscan_key,
            }
        else:
            # Native ETH transfers
            endpoint = "https://api.etherscan.io/api"
            params = {
                "module": "account",
                "action": "txlist",
                "address": "0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000000",
                "sort": "desc",
                "page": 1,
                "offset": limit,
                "apikey": self.etherscan_key,
            }

        try:
            resp = requests.get(endpoint, params=params, timeout=15)
            data = resp.json()

            if data.get("status") != "1" and data.get("message") != "No transactions found":
                logger.error(f"Etherscan error: {data}")
                return alerts

            results = data.get("result", [])
            if isinstance(results, str):
                logger.warning(f"Etherscan rate limited or error: {results}")
                return alerts

            for tx in results:
                value_eth = self._parse_eth_value(tx.get("value", "0"))
                value_usd = self._estimate_usd(value_eth)

                if value_usd < min_usd:
                    continue

                alerts.append(
                    WhaleAlert(
                        tx_hash=tx.get("hash", ""),
                        block_number=int(tx.get("blockNumber", 0)),
                        from_address=tx.get("from", ""),
                        to_address=tx.get("to", ""),
                        value_eth=value_eth,
                        value_usd=value_usd,
                        timestamp=int(tx.get("timeStamp", 0)),
                        token_symbol=tx.get("tokenSymbol"),
                        token_address=tx.get("contractAddress"),
                        is_suspicious=self._detect_suspicious(tx),
                        notes=self._get_tx_notes(tx),
                    )
                )
        except requests.RequestException as e:
            logger.error(f"Failed to fetch from Etherscan: {e}")

        return alerts

    def _fetch_via_rpc(
        self,
        token_address: Optional[str],
        min_usd: float,
        hours_back: int,
        limit: int,
    ) -> List[WhaleAlert]:
        """Fallback: use JSON-RPC for ETH transfers only (no token events without indexer)."""
        # This is a simplified fallback — full token tracking requires an indexer
        logger.warning("RPC fallback: only native ETH txs will be captured reliably")
        
        from_block_hex = self._get_block_from_timestamp(
            int(time.time()) - hours_back * 3600
        )

        payload = {
            "jsonrpc": "2.0",
            "method": "eth_getLogs",
            "params": [
                {
                    "fromBlock": from_block_hex,
                    "toBlock": "latest",
                    "address": "0x0000000000000000000000000000000000000000",  # not valid for ETH
                }
            ],
            "id": 1,
        }

        # NOTE: RPC method for ETH txs needs tx list, not logs
        # Proper fallback would use eth_blockNumber + eth_getTransactionByHash
        # For now return empty and log the limitation
        logger.info("RPC whale fetching requires an indexer like Alchemy for token transfers")
        return []

    def _estimate_usd(self, eth_value: float, eth_price: float = 3500.0) -> float:
        """Rough USD estimate — replace with real price feed for accuracy."""
        return eth_value * eth_price

    # ─── Address profiling ───────────────────────────────────────────────

    def get_address_summary(self, address: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """
        Return a summary dict for any Ethereum address.
        Uses Etherscan for basic stats.
        """
        cache_key = f"addr_summary:{address}"
        if self._is_cached(cache_key):
            return self._get_cached(cache_key)

        summary = {
            "address": address,
            "tx_count": 0,
            "total_eth_received": 0.0,
            "total_eth_sent": 0.0,
            "current_eth_balance": 0.0,
            "first_tx_time": None,
            "last_tx_time": None,
            "is_contract": False,
            "label": self._tracked_addresses.get(address.lower(), ""),
        }

        if not self.etherscan_key:
            return summary

        try:
            resp = requests.get(
                "https://api.etherscan.io/api",
                params={
                    "module": "account",
                    "action": "txlist",
                    "address": address,
                    "sort": "desc",
                    "page": 1,
                    "offset": 1,
                    "apikey": self.etherscan_key,
                },
                timeout=15,
            )
            data = resp.json()
            if data.get("status") == "1":
                txs = data.get("result", [])
                if txs:
                    summary["tx_count"] = len(txs)
                    summary["first_tx_time"] = int(txs[-1].get("timeStamp", 0))
                    summary["last_tx_time"] = int(txs[0].get("timeStamp", 0))

            # Balance
            bal_resp = requests.get(
                "https://api.etherscan.io/api",
                params={
                    "module": "account",
                    "action": "balance",
                    "address": address,
                    "tag": "latest",
                    "apikey": self.etherscan_key,
                },
                timeout=15,
            )
            bal_data = bal_resp.json()
            if bal_data.get("status") == "1":
                summary["current_eth_balance"] = self._parse_eth_value(
                    bal_data.get("result", "0")
                )

            # Contract check
            code_resp = requests.get(
                "https://api.etherscan.io/api",
                params={
                    "module": "account",
                    "action": "getsourcecode",
                    "address": address,
                    "apikey": self.etherscan_key,
                },
                timeout=15,
            )
            code_data = code_resp.json()
            if code_data.get("status") == "1":
                source = code_data.get("result", [{}])[0]
                summary["is_contract"] = bool(source.get("SourceCode"))

        except requests.RequestException as e:
            logger.error(f"Failed to fetch address summary for {address}: {e}")

        self._set_cache(cache_key, summary)
        return summary

    # ─── Exchange flow detection ────────────────────────────────────────

    def detect_exchange_flow(self, address: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """
        Heuristic: classify an address as exchange / wallet / contract.
        Uses known exchange address prefixes — not foolproof.
        """
        exchange_prefixes = {
            "0x28c6c06298d514db089934071355e5743bf21d60": "Binance 8",
            "0x21a31ee1afc51d94c2efccaa2092ad1028285549": "Binance 9",
            "0x56eddb7aa87536c09ccc2793473599fd21a8b17f": "Binance 10",
            "0x9696f8e268d1a3f6f17bb0a0e6a6a3b2c9e1d5b": "Coinbase 1",
            "0xfbb1b73c4f0bda4f67dca266ce6ef42f520f9e35": "Kraken",
        }

        addr_lower = address.lower()
        if addr_lower in exchange_prefixes:
            return {"classification": "exchange", "source": "known_list", "name": exchange_prefixes[addr_lower]}

        summary = self.get_address_summary(address)
        if summary.get("is_contract"):
            return {"classification": "contract", "source": "is_contract", "name": ""}
        if summary.get("tx_count", 0) > 10_000:
            return {"classification": "exchange_probable", "source": "high_tx_count", "name": ""}
        if summary.get("current_eth_balance", 0) > 100_000:
            return {"classification": "whale_wallet_probable", "source": "high_balance", "name": ""}

        return {"classification": "unknown", "source": "heuristic", "name": ""}

    # ─── Helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    def _parse_eth_value(self, value_str: str) -> float:
        try:
            return int(value_str) / 1e18
        except (ValueError, OverflowError):
            return 0.0

    def _get_block_from_timestamp(self, timestamp: int) -> str:
        """Approximate block number from timestamp using ~12s block time."""
        latest_block = 21_000_000  # approximate
        return hex(latest_block)

    def _detect_suspicious(self, tx: Dict) -> bool:
        """Heuristic for suspicious transaction patterns."""
        input_data = tx.get("input", "0x")
        # Non-standard input data often indicates a contract call
        if input_data != "0x" and input_data != "0x":
            return True
        return False

    def _get_tx_notes(self, tx: Dict) -> str:
        notes = []
        input_data = tx.get("input", "0x")
        if input_data and input_data != "0x":
            notes.append("contract_interaction")
        gas_used = tx.get("gasUsed", "0")
        gas_int = int(gas_used) if gas_used else 0
        if gas_int > 2_000_000:
            notes.append("high_gas")
        return ", ".join(notes) if notes else "standard_transfer"

    # ─── Cache ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    def _is_cached(self, key: str) -> bool:
        if key in self._cache:
            ts, _ = self._cache[key]
            return (time.time() - ts) < self.cache_ttl
        return False

    def _get_cached(self, key: str) -> Any:
        _, val = self._cache[key]
        return val

    def _set_cache(self, key: str, val: Any) -> None:
        self._cache[key] = (time.time(), val)
        # Simple cache size guard
        if len(self._cache) > 500:
            oldest = min(self._cache, key=lambda k: self._cache[k][0])
            del self._cache[oldest]

    def clear_cache(self) -> None:
        self._cache.clear()
"}

{"content": """"
Footprint Analytics Client — cross-chain NFT, DEX, wallet labeling data.
Docs: https://www.footprint.network/
API: https://api.footprint.network/
"""

import os
import time
import logging
from typing import Optional, List, Dict, Any

import requests

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

FOOTPRINT_API_KEY = os.getenv("FOOTPRINT_API_KEY", "")
FOOTPRINT_BASE_URL = "https://api.footprint.network"


class FootprintClient:
    """
    Query Footprint Analytics for:
    - NFT collection data and floor prices
    - DEX liquidity and trade data
    - Wallet labeling and cluster analysis
    - Cross-chain analytics (ETH, BSC, Polygon, Arbitrum, etc.)
    
    Usage:
        client = FootprintClient()
        collections = client.get_nft_collections(chain="ethereum")
        pools = client.get_dex_pools(chain="arbitrum")
        labels = client.get_wallet_labels("0x...")
    """

    def __init__(self, api_key: Optional[str] = None, cache_ttl: int = 120):
        self.api_key = api_key or FOOTPRINT_API_KEY
        self.cache_ttl = cache_ttl
        self._cache: Dict[str, tuple[float, Any]] = {}

    # ─── NFT Data ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    def get_nft_collections(
        self,
        chain: str = "ethereum",
        limit: int = 50,
        sort_by: str = "volume",
    ) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """
        Get top NFT collections by volume / floor price.
        
        Args:
            chain: ethereum | bs | polygon | arbitrum | optimism
            limit: max collections to return
            sort_by: volume | floor_price | sales_count
        """
        cache_key = f"nft_collections:{chain}:{sort_by}:{limit}"
        if self._is_cached(cache_key):
            return self._get_cached(cache_key)

        if not self.api_key:
            logger.warning("Footprint API key required — set FOOTPRINT_API_KEY")
            return []

        try:
            resp = requests.post(
                f"{FOOTPRINT_BASE_URL}/v1/NFT/CollectionRanking",
                json={
                    "chain": chain,
                    "sort_by": sort_by,
                    "limit": limit,
                },
                headers=self._headers(),
                timeout=15,
            )
            resp.raise_for_status()
            raw = resp.json()
            collections = raw.get("data", [])
            normalized = [self._normalize_collection(c) for c in collections]
            self._set_cache(cache_key, normalized)
            return normalized

        except requests.RequestException as e:
            logger.error(f"Footprint NFT collections fetch failed: {e}")
            return []

    def get_nft_collection(
        self,
        collection_slug: str,
        chain: str = "ethereum",
    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Get detailed data for a specific NFT collection."""
        cache_key = f"nft_collection:{collection_slug}:{chain}"
        if self._is_cached(cache_key):
            return self._get_cached(cache_key)

        if not self.api_key:
            return {}

        try:
            resp = requests.post(
                f"{FOOTPRINT_BASE_URL}/v1/NFT/CollectionDetail",
                json={
                    "chain": chain,
                    "slug": collection_slug,
                },
                headers=self._headers(),
                timeout=15,
            )
            resp.raise_for_status()
            raw = resp.json()
            data = raw.get("data", {})
            normalized = self._normalize_collection(data)
            self._set_cache(cache_key, normalized)
            return normalized

        except requests.RequestException as e:
            logger.error(f"Footprint NFT collection detail failed: {e}")
            return {}

    def get_nft_floor_price_history(
        self,
        collection_slug: str,
        chain: str = "ethereum",
        days: int = 30,
    ) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """Get floor price history for a collection."""
        cache_key = f"nft_floor:{collection_slug}:{chain}:{days}"
        if self._is_cached(cache_key):
            return self._get_cached(cache_key)

        if not self.api_key:
            return []

        try:
            resp = requests.post(
                f"{FOOTPRINT_BASE_URL}/v1/NFT/CollectionFloorPrice",
                json={
                    "chain": chain,
                    "slug": collection_slug,
                    "days": days,
                },
                headers=self._headers(),
                timeout=15,
            )
            resp.raise_for_status()
            raw = resp.json()
            data = raw.get("data", [])
            self._set_cache(cache_key, data)
            return data

        except requests.RequestException as e:
            logger.error(f"Footprint floor price history failed: {e}")
            return []

    # ─── DEX Data ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    def get_dex_pools(
        self,
        chain: str = "ethereum",
        protocol: Optional[str] = None,
        limit: int = 100,
    ) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """
        Get DEX liquidity pools.
        
        Args:
            chain: ethereum | bs | polygon | arbitrum | optimism | avalanche
            protocol: Uniswap | SushiSwap | Curve | GMX | dYdX | None for all
            limit: max pools
        """
        cache_key = f"dex_pools:{chain}:{protocol}:{limit}"
        if self._is_cached(cache_key):
            return self._get_cached(cache_key)

        if not self.api_key:
            logger.warning("Footprint API key required")
            return []

        body: Dict[str, Any] = {"chain": chain, "limit": limit}
        if protocol:
            body["protocol"] = protocol

        try:
            resp = requests.post(
                f"{FOOTPRINT_BASE_URL}/v1/DEX/Pools",
                json=body,
                headers=self._headers(),
                timeout=15,
            )
            resp.raise_for_status()
            raw = resp.json()
            pools = raw.get("data", [])
            normalized = [self._normalize_pool(p) for p in pools]
            self._set_cache(cache_key, normalized)
            return normalized

        except requests.RequestException as e:
            logger.error(f"Footprint DEX pools fetch failed: {e}")
            return []

    def get_dex_trades(
        self,
        chain: str = "ethereum",
        protocol: Optional[str] = None,
        token_address: Optional[str] = None,
        limit: int = 100,
    ) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """
        Get recent DEX trades.
        
        Args:
            chain: blockchain
            protocol: DEX name
            token_address: filter by token
            limit: max trades
        """
        cache_key = f"dex_trades:{chain}:{protocol}:{token_address}:{limit}"
        if self._is_cached(cache_key):
            return self._get_cached(cache_key)

        if not self.api_key:
            return []

        body: Dict[str, Any] = {"chain": chain, "limit": limit}
        if protocol:
            body["protocol"] = protocol
        if token_address:
            body["token_address"] = token_address

        try:
            resp = requests.post(
                f"{FOOTPRINT_BASE_URL}/v1/DEX/TraderBatch",
                json=body,
                headers=self._headers(),
                timeout=15,
            )
            resp.raise_for_status()
            raw = resp.json()
            trades = raw.get("data", [])
            normalized = [self._normalize_trade(t) for t in trades]
            self._set_cache(cache_key, normalized)
            return normalized

        except requests.RequestException as e:
            logger.error(f"Footprint DEX trades fetch failed: {e}")
            return []

    # ─── Wallet Labeling ──────────────────────────────────────────────────

    def get_wallet_labels(
        self,
        address: str,
    ) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """
        Get known labels/tags for a wallet address.
        
        Returns list of label objects with:
          - label: str (e.g., "Smart Money", "Whale", "DeFi User")
          - source: str (e.g., "footprint", "user_tag")
          - confidence: float 0-1
        """
        cache_key = f"wallet_labels:{address}"
        if self._is_cached(cache_key):
            return self._get_cached(cache_key)

        if not self.api_key:
            return []

        try:
            resp = requests.post(
                f"{FOOTPRINT_BASE_URL}/v1/Wallet/AddressLabel",
                json={"address": address},
                headers=self._headers(),
                timeout=15,
            )
            resp.raise_for_status()
            raw = resp.json()
            labels = raw.get("data", [])
            self._set_cache(cache_key, labels)
            return labels

        except requests.RequestException as e:
            logger.error(f"Footprint wallet labels fetch failed: {e}")
            return []

    def get_wallet_clusters(
        self,
        address: str,
    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """
        Get wallet cluster analysis — identifies related wallets.
        
        Returns:
          - cluster_id
          - related_addresses
          - cluster_size
          - cluster_type (e.g., "exchange", "defi", "nft")
        """
        cache_key = f"wallet_clusters:{address}"
        if self._is_cached(cache_key):
            return self._get_cached(cache_key)

        if not self.api_key:
            return {}

        try:
            resp = requests.post(
                f"{FOOTPRINT_BASE_URL}/v1/Wallet/WalletCluster",
                json={"address": address},
                headers=self._headers(),
                timeout=15,
            )
            resp.raise_for_status()
            raw = resp.json()
            data = raw.get("data", {})
            self._set_cache(cache_key, data)
            return data

        except requests.RequestException as e:
            logger.error(f"Footprint wallet cluster failed: {e}")
            return {}

    def get_smart_money_addresses(
        self,
        chain: str = "ethereum",
        min_pnl: float = 10_000,
        limit: int = 50,
    ) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """
        Get known smart money addresses (profitability-based).
        
        Returns addresses with their estimated P&L.
        """
        cache_key = f"smart_money:{chain}:{min_pnl}:{limit}"
        if self._is_cached(cache_key):
            return self._get_cached(cache_key)

        if not self.api_key:
            return []

        try:
            resp = requests.post(
                f"{FOOTPRINT_BASE_URL}/v1/Wallet/SmartMoneyList",
                json={
                    "chain": chain,
                    "min_pnl": min_pnl,
                    "limit": limit,
                },
                headers=self._headers(),
                timeout=15,
            )
            resp.raise_for_status()
            raw = resp.json()
            wallets = raw.get("data", [])
            self._set_cache(cache_key, wallets)
            return wallets

        except requests.RequestException as e:
            logger.error(f"Footprint smart money list failed: {e}")
            return []

    # ─── Normalizers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    def _normalize_collection(self, c: Dict) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        return {
            "slug": c.get("slug", ""),
            "name": c.get("name", ""),
            "chain": c.get("chain", ""),
            "floor_price": c.get("floor_price", 0.0),
            "floor_price_eth": c.get("floor_price_eth", 0.0),
            "volume_24h": c.get("volume_24h", 0.0),
            "volume_7d": c.get("volume_7d", 0.0),
            "sales_24h": c.get("sales_24h", 0),
            "owners": c.get("owners", 0),
            "total_supply": c.get("total_supply", 0),
            "market_cap": c.get("market_cap", 0.0),
        }

    def _normalize_pool(self, p: Dict) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        return {
            "protocol": p.get("protocol", ""),
            "chain": p.get("chain", ""),
            "pair": p.get("pair", ""),
            "token_0": p.get("token_0", ""),
            "token_1": p.get("token_1", ""),
            "reserve0": p.get("reserve0", 0.0),
            "reserve1": p.get("reserve1", 0.0),
            "volume_24h": p.get("volume_24h", 0.0),
            "fee_24h": p.get("fee_24h", 0.0),
            "liquidity": p.get("liquidity", 0.0),
        }

    def _normalize_trade(self, t: Dict) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        return {
            "tx_hash": t.get("tx_hash", ""),
            "block_number": t.get("block_number", 0),
            "timestamp": t.get("block_timestamp", 0),
            "trader": t.get("trader", ""),
            "chain": t.get("chain", ""),
            "protocol": t.get("protocol", ""),
            "token_in": t.get("token_in", ""),
            "token_out": t.get("token_out", ""),
            "amount_in": t.get("amount_in", 0.0),
            "amount_out": t.get("amount_out", 0.0),
            "price": t.get("price", 0.0),
            "side": t.get("side", ""),  # buy | sell
        }

    # ─── Helpers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    def _headers(self) -> Dict[str, str]:
        return {

"""
DEX Data Provider — Uniswap/SushiSwap/Curve trade flow and liquidity analytics.
Uses The Graph subgraphs + direct pool queries.

Supports:
  - Uniswap v2 / v3 (Ethereum + L2s)
  - SushiSwap
  - Curve Finance
  - GMX (Avalanche)
"""

import os
import time
import logging
from typing import Optional, List, Dict, Any
from dataclasses import dataclass

import requests

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

THEGRAPH_API_KEY = os.getenv("THEGRAPH_API_KEY", "")
UNISWAP_V3_SUBGRAPH = "https://api.thegraph.com/subgraphs/name/uniswap/uniswap-v3"
UNISWAP_V2_SUBGRAPH = "https://api.thegraph.com/subgraphs/name/uniswap/uniswap-v2"
SUSHISWAP_SUBGRAPH = "https://api.thegraph.com/subgraphs/name/sushiswap/exchange"


@dataclass
class DexTrade:
    """A single DEX trade."""
    tx_hash: str
    timestamp: int
    block_number: int
    trader: str
    token_in: str
    token_out: str
    amount_in: float
    amount_out: float
    amount_in_usd: float
    amount_out_usd: float
    price: float
    side: str  # BUY or SELL
    pool: str
    protocol: str
    chain: str = "ethereum"


@dataclass
class PoolLiquidity:
    """Liquidity state of a DEX pool."""
    pool_address: str
    token_0: str
    token_1: str
    reserve_0: float
    reserve_1: float
    liquidity: float
    volume_24h: float
    fee_24h: float
    protocol: str
    chain: str = "ethereum"


class DexDataProvider:
    """
    Fetch DEX trade data and liquidity from The Graph subgraphs.
    
    Usage:
        provider = DexDataProvider()
        trades = provider.get_trades(token_address="0x...", limit=100)
        pools = provider.get_top_pools(protocol="uniswap-v3", limit=50)
        flow = provider.get_buy_sell_pressure("0x...")
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        thegraph_key: Optional[str] = None,
        cache_ttl: int = 60,
    ):
        self.thegraph_key = thegraph_key or THEGRAPH_API_KEY
        self.cache_ttl = cache_ttl
        self._cache: Dict[str, tuple[float, Any]] = {}

    # ─── Trade data ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    def get_trades(
        self,
        token_address: str,
        protocol: str = "uniswap-v3",
        chain: str = "ethereum",
        limit: int = 100,
    ) -> List[DexTrade]:
        """
        Fetch recent swaps for a token.
        
        Args:
            token_address: ERC20 token address (checksummed)
            protocol: uniswap-v3 | uniswap-v2 | sushiswap | curve
            chain: ethereum | polygon | arbitrum | avalanche
            limit: max trades (capped at 1000)
        """
        cache_key = f"dex_trades:{protocol}:{chain}:{token_address}:{limit}"
        if self._is_cached(cache_key):
            return self._get_cached(cache_key)

        subgraph_url = self._get_subgraph_url(protocol, chain)
        if not subgraph_url:
            logger.error(f"No subgraph for protocol={protocol}, chain={chain}")
            return []

        query = self._build_trades_query(token_address, limit)
        try:
            resp = requests.post(subgraph_url, json={"query": query}, timeout=20)
            resp.raise_for_status()
            raw = resp.json()

            if "errors" in raw:
                logger.error(f"GraphQL errors: {raw['errors']}")
                return []

            swaps = raw.get("data", {}).get("swaps", [])
            trades = [self._normalize_swap(s, protocol, chain) for s in swaps]
            self._set_cache(cache_key, trades)
            return trades

        except requests.RequestException as e:
            logger.error(f"DEX trades fetch failed: {e}")
            return []

    def get_buy_sell_pressure(
        self,
        token_address: str,
        protocol: str = "uniswap-v3",
        chain: str = "ethereum",
        hours: int = 24,
    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """
        Calculate buy/sell pressure over a time window.
        
        Returns:
          - buy_count, sell_count
          - buy_volume_usd, sell_volume_usd
          - net_flow_usd (positive = net buying)
          - buy_sell_ratio
        """
        # Fetch enough trades to cover the window
        limit = min(1000, hours * 60)  # rough estimate
        trades = self.get_trades(token_address, protocol, chain, limit)

        cutoff = int(time.time()) - hours * 3600
        recent = [t for t in trades if t.timestamp >= cutoff]

        buy_count = sum(1 for t in recent if t.side == "BUY")
        sell_count = sum(1 for t in recent if t.side == "SELL")
        buy_volume = sum(t.amount_in_usd for t in recent if t.side == "BUY")
        sell_volume = sum(t.amount_in_usd for t in recent if t.side == "SELL")
        net_flow = buy_volume - sell_volume

        return {
            "token": token_address,
            "protocol": protocol,
            "window_hours": hours,
            "buy_count": buy_count,
            "sell_count": sell_count,
            "buy_volume_usd": buy_volume,
            "sell_volume_usd": sell_volume,
            "net_flow_usd": net_flow,
            "buy_sell_ratio": buy_volume / sell_volume if sell_volume > 0 else float("inf"),
            "trade_count": len(recent),
        }

    def get_token_flow(
        self,
        token_address: str,
        hours: int = 24,
    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """
        Aggregate buy/sell pressure across major DEXes for a token.
        Checks Uniswap v3, v2, and SushiSwap.
        """
        combined = {
            "buy_count": 0, "sell_count": 0,
            "buy_volume_usd": 0.0, "sell_volume_usd": 0.0,
            "net_flow_usd": 0.0,
            "by_protocol": {},
        }

        for protocol in ["uniswap-v3", "uniswap-v2", "sushiswap"]:
            pressure = self.get_buy_sell_pressure(token_address, protocol, "ethereum", hours)
            combined["buy_count"] += pressure["buy_count"]
            combined["sell_count"] += pressure["sell_count"]
            combined["buy_volume_usd"] += pressure["buy_volume_usd"]
            combined["sell_volume_usd"] += pressure["sell_volume_usd"]
            combined["net_flow_usd"] += pressure["net_flow_usd"]
            combined["by_protocol"][protocol] = pressure

        return combined

    # ─── Pool data ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    def get_top_pools(
        self,
        token_address: Optional[str] = None,
        protocol: str = "uniswap-v3",
        chain: str = "ethereum",
        limit: int = 50,
    ) -> List[PoolLiquidity]:
        """
        Get top pools by 24h volume.
        
        Args:
            token_address: Optional filter to get pools containing this token
            protocol: DEX protocol
            chain: blockchain
            limit: max pools
        """
        cache_key = f"dex_pools:{protocol}:{chain}:{token_address}:{limit}"
        if self._is_cached(cache_key):
            return self._get_cached(cache_key)

        subgraph_url = self._get_subgraph_url(protocol, chain)
        if not subgraph_url:
            return []

        query = self._build_pools_query(token_address, limit)
        try:
            resp = requests.post(subgraph_url, json={"query": query}, timeout=20)
            resp.raise_for_status()
            raw = resp.json()

            if "errors" in raw:
                logger.error(f"GraphQL errors: {raw['errors']}")
                return []

            pools_raw = (
                raw.get("data", {}).get("pools", []) or
                raw.get("data", {}).get("pairDayDatas", [])
            )
            pools = [self._normalize_pool(p, protocol, chain) for p in pools_raw]
            self._set_cache(cache_key, pools)
            return pools

        except requests.RequestException as e:
            logger.error(f"DEX pools fetch failed: {e}")
            return []

    # ─── Subgraph URL routing ─────────────────────────────────────────────

    def _get_subgraph_url(self, protocol: str, chain: str) -> Optional[str]:
        """Map protocol + chain to The Graph subgraph endpoint."""
        if chain != "ethereum":
            # L2 subgraphs use different endpoints
            l2_urls = {
                ("uniswap-v3", "polygon"):     "https://api.thegraph.com/subgraphs/name/ianlapham/polygon-uniswap-v3",
                ("uniswap-v3", "arbitrum"):    "https://api.thegraph.com/subgraphs/name/ianlapham/arbitrum-uniswap-v3",
                ("uniswap-v3", "optimism"):    "https://api.thegraph.com/subgraphs/name/ianlapham/optimism-uniswap-v3",
                ("uniswap-v3", "avalanche"):   "https://api.thegraph.com/subgraphs/name/ianlapham/avalanche-uniswap-v3",
                ("sushiswap", "polygon"):      "https://api.thegraph.com/subgraphs/name/sushiswap/matic-exchange",
                ("sushiswap", "arbitrum"):     "https://api.thegraph.com/subgraphs/name/sushiswap/arbitrum-exchange",
                ("sushiswap", "avalanche"):    "https://api.thegraph.com/subgraphs/name/sushiswap/avalanche-exchange",
            }
            key = (protocol, chain)
            return l2_urls.get(key)

        urls = {
            "uniswap-v3":  UNISWAP_V3_SUBGRAPH,
            "uniswap-v2":  UNISWAP_V2_SUBGRAPH,
            "sushiswap":   SUSHISWAP_SUBGRAPH,
        }
        return urls.get(protocol)

    # ─── GraphQL queries ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

    def _build_trades_query(self, token_address: str, limit: int) -> str:
        return f"""
        {{
          swaps(
            first: {limit}
            orderBy: timestamp
            orderDirection: desc
            where: {{ token_in: "{token_address}" }}
          ) {{
            id
            timestamp
            blockNumber
            transaction {{ id }}
            sender
            tokenIn {{ id symbol decimals }}
            tokenOut {{ id symbol decimals }}
            amountIn
            amountOut
            amountInUSD
            amountOutUSD
          }}
          swaps2: swaps(
            first: {limit}
            orderBy: timestamp
            orderDirection: desc
            where: {{ token_out: "{token_address}" }}
          ) {{
            id
            timestamp
            blockNumber
            transaction {{ id }}
            sender
            tokenIn {{ id symbol decimals }}
            tokenOut {{ id symbol decimals }}
            amountIn
            amountOut
            amountInUSD
            amountOutUSD
          }}
        }}
        """

    def _build_pools_query(self, token_address: Optional[str], limit: int) -> str:
        token_filter = f'token0: "{token_address}"' if token_address else ""
        return f"""
        {{
          pools(
            first: {limit}
            orderBy: volumeUSD
            orderDirection: desc
            where: {{{token_filter}}}
          ) {{
            id
            token0 {{ id symbol decimals }}
            token1 {{ id symbol decimals }}
            reserve0
            reserve1
            volumeUSD
            feeUSD
            liquidity
          }}
        }}
        """

    # ─── Normalizers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    def _normalize_swap(self, s: Dict, protocol: str, chain: str) -> DexTrade:
        token_in = s.get("tokenIn", {})
        token_out = s.get("tokenOut", {})

        amount_in = self._parse_token_amount(
            s.get("amountIn", "0"),
            token_in.get("decimals", 18),
        )
        amount_out = self._parse_token_amount(
            s.get("amountOut", "0"),
            token_out.get("decimals", 18),
        )
        amount_in_usd = float(s.get("amountInUSD", 0) or 0)
        amount_out_usd = float(s.get("amountOutUSD", 0) or 0)

        # Determine side: if token_in is the target token, it's a SELL (selling the token)
        # This is simplified — for proper classification you need price vs fair price
        side = "BUY" if amount_in_usd > amount_out_usd else "SELL"

        tx_hash = ""
        if isinstance(s.get("transaction"), dict):
            tx_hash = s["transaction"].get("id", "")

        return DexTrade(
            tx_hash=tx_hash,
            timestamp=int(s.get("timestamp", 0)),
            block_number=int(s.get("blockNumber", 0)),
            trader=s.get("sender", ""),
            token_in=token_in.get("id", ""),
            token_out=token_out.get("id", ""),
            amount_in=amount_in,
            amount_out=amount_out,
            amount_in_usd=amount_in_usd,
            amount_out_usd=amount_out_usd,
            price=amount_out_usd / amount_in if amount_in > 0 else 0.0,
            side=side,
            pool=s.get("id", ""),
            protocol=protocol,
            chain=chain,
        )

    def _normalize_pool(self, p: Dict, protocol: str, chain: str) -> PoolLiquidity:
        token_0 = p.get("token0", {})
        token_1 = p.get("token1", {})

        return PoolLiquidity(
            pool_address=p.get("id", ""),
            token_0=token_0.get("id", ""),
            token_1=token_1.get("id", ""),
            reserve_0=float(p.get("reserve0", 0) or 0),
            reserve_1=float(p.get("reserve1", 0) or 0),
            liquidity=float(p.get("liquidity", 0) or 0),
            volume_24h=float(p.get("volumeUSD", 0) or 0),
            fee_24h=float(p.get("feeUSD", 0) or 0),
            protocol=protocol,
            chain=chain,
        )

    def _parse_token_amount(self, value_str: str, decimals: int) -> float:
        try:
            value = int(value_str)
            return value / (10 ** decimals)
        except (ValueError, OverflowError):
            return 0.0

    # ─── Sandwich attack detection ───────────────────────────────────────

    def detect_sandwich(
        self,
        tx_hash: str,
        chain: str = "ethereum",
    ) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """
        Heuristic sandwich attack detection.
        Looks for three consecutive txs: same token pair, same block,
        with the attacker's tx in the middle at higher gas.
        
        Note: Requires archive node or specific indexer for full detection.
        This is a simplified heuristic using public data.
        """
        # Simplified — full detection needs mempool data
        return {
            "tx_hash": tx_hash,
            "is_sandwich": False,
            "confidence": "low",
            "note": "Full sandwich detection requires Flashbots or archival node access",
        }

    # ─── Cache ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    def _is_cached(self, key: str) -> bool:
        if key in self._cache:
            ts, _ = self._cache[key]
            return (time.time() - ts) < self.cache_ttl
        return False

    def _get_cached(self, key: str) -> Any:
        return self._cache[key][1]

    def _set_cache(self, key: str, val: Any) -> None:
        self._cache[key] = (time.time(), val)
        if len(self._cache) > 500:
            oldest = min(self._cache, key=lambda k: self._cache[k][0])
            del self._cache[oldest]

    def clear_cache(self) -> None:
        self._cache.clear()
